title: Structured Groom proposals
outcome: Teams can approve and hand off one auditable product contract.

decision_brief.problem: Proposal twins drift and prose status can be mistaken for approval.
decision_brief.recommendation: Use one structured proposal with generated projections and hash-bound approval.
decision_brief.why_now: The next workhorse models need a provider-neutral execution contract.

audience.name: Product delivery team
audience.description: People defining, approving, and implementing product work.

jobs_to_be_done.situation: When a proposal is ready for delivery
jobs_to_be_done.motivation: I want to approve exact reviewed content
jobs_to_be_done.outcome: so downstream work cannot consume stale intent.

evidence.summary: The current Markdown and HTML twins can drift.

assumptions.statement: Machine consumers can read the canonical JSON directly.
assumptions.validation: Exercise RFC and Dev readers against the fixture.

in_scope.statement: A strict proposal schema and checker.

requirements.statement: Approval must bind the exact proposal bytes and revision.

acceptance_criteria.then: the audit binds its exact bytes, content hash, revision, and approver

design_requirements.requirement: Show lifecycle, revision, approval state, and open decisions visibly.

alternatives.name: Keep hand-edited Markdown and HTML twins
alternatives.reason_rejected: It preserves drift and ambiguous approval.

risks.risk: Strict validation could strand legacy proposals.
risks.mitigation: Keep a bounded inspection-only legacy reader.

resolved_decisions.question: What is the canonical proposal source?
resolved_decisions.rationale: It supports deterministic projections and machine handoff.

question_reviews.question: Is the problem and evidence chain sufficient?

presentation.summary: One source, explicit approval, bounded handoff.
